site.name: CryptoBets.com
site.tagline: The Most Trusted Crypto Gambling Guide
site.description: CryptoBets.com is the definitive guide to crypto casinos, sportsbooks, poker rooms, exchanges, and wallets. We test every platform with real cryptocurrency deposits so you get accurate, unbiased reviews.

scoring_weights.description: Weighted formula used to calculate the overall rating for casino and sportsbook brands

authors.name: Marcus Chen
authors.title: Editor-in-Chief
authors.bio: Marcus has been covering crypto gambling since 2018, having personally tested over 200 platforms. A former software engineer turned gambling journalist, he brings a technical edge to every review — dissecting provably fair algorithms, smart contract audits, and withdrawal mechanics. He holds certifications in responsible gambling and has spoken at blockchain gaming conferences in Singapore and Prague.

authors.name: Elena Volkov
authors.title: Senior Exchange Analyst
authors.bio: Elena is a blockchain developer and former quantitative analyst who transitioned to crypto journalism in 2020. She has built DeFi smart contracts, audited exchange security protocols, and managed crypto portfolios for high-net-worth clients. Her exchange reviews go beyond surface metrics — she tests API latency, liquidity depth, and security incident response times firsthand.

authors.name: James Okonkwo
authors.title: Poker & Gaming Specialist
authors.bio: James is a semi-professional poker player who discovered Bitcoin poker rooms in 2019 and never looked back. With over $500,000 in lifetime tournament earnings across both fiat and crypto poker sites, he evaluates platforms from a seasoned player's perspective — game selection, rake fairness, tournament structures, and soft field dynamics. He also covers crypto casino table games, slots, and live dealer with a player-first mindset.

authors.name: Sarah Nakamura
authors.title: Testing Lead & Withdrawal Speed Specialist
authors.bio: Sarah is CryptoBets' dedicated testing lead who processes real deposits and withdrawals on every platform reviewed. She maintains a proprietary database of 1,000+ timed withdrawal tests across BTC, ETH, and USDT on over 50 platforms. Her meticulous approach includes testing support response times, KYC friction, and bonus claim processes. Sarah also manages our wallet security testing lab, evaluating hardware wallets against physical and software attack vectors.

Models range from $79 (Nano S Plus) to $279 (Ledger Stax with E-Ink touchscreen).

Trusted by over 2 million users worldwide, Trezor's entire codebase (firmware, software, and hardware) is 100% open-source and continuously audited by the global security community. Advanced security features include Shamir Backup, native Tor support, CoinJoin privacy for Bitcoin, and quantum-ready cryptography on the Safe 7. The 'Don't Trust, Verify' ethos makes Trezor the choice of security purists.

comparisons.title: Stake.com vs BC.Game: Which Crypto Casino Wins in 2026?
comparisons.summary: Stake.com leads in brand recognition, sportsbook depth, and withdrawal speed. BC.Game counters with 150+ cryptocurrency support, a larger 10,000+ game library, and no mandatory KYC. High-rollers prefer Stake's VIP host program; crypto maximalists prefer BC.Game's coin variety.
comparisons.winnerReason: Stake wins on brand trust, sportsbook comprehensiveness, and withdrawal speed, while BC.Game is better for maximum crypto variety and anonymous play.
comparisons.author: marcus-chen

comparisons.title: Stake.com vs Cloudbet: Best Crypto Sportsbook in 2026?
comparisons.summary: Stake wins on game variety and withdrawal speed. Cloudbet counters with a market-leading 5 BTC welcome bonus, no wagering cash rewards, and the industry's longest track record (est. 2013 vs. 2017).
comparisons.winnerReason: Stake's larger game library and faster withdrawals edge out Cloudbet, though high-stakers should consider Cloudbet's 5 BTC bonus.
comparisons.author: sarah-nakamura

comparisons.title: BitStarz vs mBit Casino: Award-Winner vs Game Giant 2026
comparisons.summary: BitStarz is the award-winning veteran (est. 2014) with stronger brand trust and a superior VIP program. mBit Casino counters with a larger 8,800+ game library and RTP transparency on every game tile.
comparisons.winnerReason: BitStarz's multi-award winning reputation, stronger VIP program, and more consistent player experience earn it the edge, though mBit is the better choice for slots purists who value game variety.
comparisons.author: marcus-chen

comparisons.title: Kraken vs Bybit: Security First vs Derivatives Power 2026
comparisons.summary: Kraken wins on security (best track record, Proof of Reserves since 2014, multi-jurisdiction regulation). Bybit dominates derivatives trading with $54B daily volume, 2,273+ assets, and ultra-low futures fees.
comparisons.winnerReason: For security-first investors and compliance-conscious traders, Kraken is unmatched. For derivatives and altcoin traders, Bybit's superior liquidity and product depth wins.
comparisons.author: elena-volkov

comparisons.title: Binance vs Coinbase: The Ultimate Exchange Comparison 2026
comparisons.summary: Binance offers dramatically lower fees (0.10% vs up to 1.49%), 350+ vs 200+ assets, and the world's highest liquidity. Coinbase wins on US regulation, FDIC insurance, and beginner accessibility. The choice between saving on fees vs. trusting a regulated public company.
comparisons.winnerReason: Binance's lower fees, higher liquidity, and broader asset selection make it the better trading platform globally, while Coinbase wins for US compliance and beginners.
comparisons.author: elena-volkov

comparisons.title: Ledger vs Trezor: Which Hardware Wallet Should You Buy in 2026?
comparisons.summary: Ledger wins on asset support (5,500 vs 1,000), Bluetooth convenience, Ledger Live's feature richness, and the EAL6+ Secure Element chip. Trezor wins on open-source transparency, Shamir Backup, native privacy (Tor, CoinJoin), and philosophy for security purists.
comparisons.winnerReason: Ledger wins for most users on asset support, ecosystem features, and convenience. Trezor wins for Bitcoin maxis and security purists who demand fully auditable, open-source code.
comparisons.author: sarah-nakamura

comparisons.title: CoinPoker vs SWC Poker: Best Crypto Poker 2026
comparisons.summary: CoinPoker is the largest dedicated crypto poker room with massive CSOP tournaments, 33% rakeback, a rebuilt platform, and Curacao licensing. SWC wins on anonymity (zero KYC), industry-lowest rake (0.75%-3%), mixed game variety, and up to 50% rakeback for loyal grinders.
comparisons.winnerReason: CoinPoker wins for most crypto poker players on tournament action, liquidity, and platform quality. SWC wins for the most privacy-conscious, mixed-game, and highest rakeback seekers.
comparisons.author: james-okonkwo

comparisons.title: Sportsbet.io vs Thunderpick: Best Crypto Sportsbook 2026
comparisons.summary: Sportsbet.io leads on general sports coverage (35+ markets), Arsenal FC sponsorship, and overall brand size. Thunderpick dominates esports betting (CS2, Dota 2, LoL, Valorant) with dedicated markets and live streaming.
comparisons.winnerReason: Sportsbet.io is the better overall crypto sportsbook. Thunderpick is the winner for esports-specific betting.

comparisons.title: Roobet vs BitStarz: Two Top Crypto Casinos Head to Head
comparisons.summary: Both are SiGMA-winning casinos. Roobet wins on instant withdrawals and UX simplicity. BitStarz wins on game library (7,100 vs 7,000+), welcome bonus size (5 BTC vs no deposit match), and weekly tournaments.
comparisons.winnerReason: BitStarz's larger welcome bonus and stronger VIP tournament structure make it the better choice for most players, while Roobet wins on withdrawal speed and UI simplicity.

comparisons.title: MetaMask vs Exodus: Best Crypto Wallet for 2026?
comparisons.summary: MetaMask dominates DeFi and Web3 access with 10,000+ dApp integrations and all EVM network support. Exodus wins on multi-chain support (Bitcoin, Solana, and 128+ assets), UI beauty, and portfolio management features.
